key_insight: The NMO Benchmark replaces drug-like proxy goals with quantum-simulation-based nanotechnology targets, exposing that simple, scientifically grounded methods can outperform popular generative optimization systems.
engineering_relevance: For materials discovery, NMO provides a more realistic evaluation and optimization framework for designing molecules with nanotechnology-relevant properties rather than benchmark-specific drug metrics.
method: Built a benchmark suite with quantum simulations as the oracle instead of proxy property predictors
Added strict protocols and hard structural constraints to prioritize scientific utility over leaderboard overfitting
Introduced a new baseline using structural-constraint-aware representations plus domain-agnostic pretraining to reduce pharmaceutical dataset bias
evidence_signal: Across the NMO tasks, advanced molecular optimization methods underperformed simpler approaches, while the new baseline surpassed state-of-the-art physical-property results and uncovered previously unknown structural motifs.
watch_item: Because the tasks are physics-based with rugged landscapes and strict constraints, performance may depend heavily on accurate simulation setup and careful representation of structural feasibility.
paper_title: Beyond Drug Discovery: The Nanotechnology Molecular Optimization (NMO) Benchmark
